sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports knee pain following a fall while changing a light bulb, specifically in the inside of his knee. He states the pain is exacerbated by forward bending and twisting the knee. He has been applying ice and taking ibuprofen with minimal relief. He reports no other injuries.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals tenderness over the medial aspect of the right knee, particularly with valgus stressing. Sensation is intact. X-ray shows no fractures or dislocations, but demonstrates some joint effusion. No gross instability observed.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has an acute medial meniscus sprain or strain of the right knee.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order a knee brace and crutches for temporary use. Recommend ibuprofen 800 mg every 6 hours as needed for pain. Provide a two-week supply. Schedule a follow-up appointment in one week to reassess and consider an MRI if symptoms do not improve.
